body.notes:
## Updated 2026-04-19 with Akien's dictation (recovering 2026-04-18 conversation)

SwarmManager lives in the **utility closet** (`lab/utility_closet/swarm_manager.py`).

## What it does

- Houses the machines info: who's up, ollama availability, inference capability, in-use status, etc. Replaces/absorbs existing MachinesWatcher + machine_manager query patterns.
- Aggregates SystemProxy snapshots from all boxes (local + remote over SSH or UC API).
- Provides fleet-wide view — best routing target, who's offline, CPU load across fleet.
- **Absorbs T-remote-igor-bootstrap**: accepts "here's a new box, here's the SSH creds" directive, clones the repo, runs the Igor installer endpoint, launches Igor.

## Bootstrap workflow (from T-remote-igor-bootstrap, rolled in here)

1. Caller hands SwarmManager a new box entry: hostname, IP, SSH auth.
2. SwarmManager clones the repo to that box.
3. Runs the Igor installer endpoint, which installs deps + sets up env.
4. Launches Igor on the new box.
5. New instance registers back into the swarm via the home DB / UC.

## Instance-ID allocation

The installer today asks the instance_id + home_db_url when env vars aren't set. SwarmManager uses the same pattern: reaches back to the user via Igor ("what's the instance_id for this new box?") rather than auto-assigning. Keeps the naming human-intentional.

## API shape (rough)

- `swarm_manager.fleet_status()` → per-machine health + availability
- `swarm_manager.best_inference_target(criteria)` → hostname
- `swarm_manager.add_box(hostname, ssh_auth)` → bootstrap flow
- `swarm_manager.machines()` → the whole registry

## Prereq
SystemProxy facade on each box (shipped 04-16, T-uc-system-proxy-shelf).

## Provenance
Akien dictation, 2026-04-19.
body.title: SwarmManager — aggregate SystemProxy reports across machines
body.description:
## Updated 2026-04-19 with Akien's dictation (recovering 2026-04-18 conversation)

SwarmManager lives in the **utility closet** (`lab/utility_closet/swarm_manager.py`).

## What it does

- Houses the machines info: who's up, ollama availability, inference capability, in-use status, etc. Replaces/absorbs existing MachinesWatcher + machine_manager query patterns.
- Aggregates SystemProxy snapshots from all boxes (local + remote over SSH or UC API).
- Provides fleet-wide view — best routing target, who's offline, CPU load across fleet.
- **Absorbs T-remote-igor-bootstrap**: accepts "here's a new box, here's the SSH creds" directive, clones the repo, runs the Igor installer endpoint, launches Igor.

## Bootstrap workflow (from T-remote-igor-bootstrap, rolled in here)

1. Caller hands SwarmManager a new box entry: hostname, IP, SSH auth.
2. SwarmManager clones the repo to that box.
3. Runs the Igor installer endpoint, which installs deps + sets up env.
4. Launches Igor on the new box.
5. New instance registers back into the swarm via the home DB / UC.

## Instance-ID allocation

The installer today asks the instance_id + home_db_url when env vars aren't set. SwarmManager uses the same pattern: reaches back to the user via Igor ("what's the instance_id for this new box?") rather than auto-assigning. Keeps the naming human-intentional.

## API shape (rough)

- `swarm_manager.fleet_status()` → per-machine health + availability
- `swarm_manager.best_inference_target(criteria)` → hostname
- `swarm_manager.add_box(hostname, ssh_auth)` → bootstrap flow
- `swarm_manager.machines()` → the whole registry

## Prereq
SystemProxy facade on each box (shipped 04-16, T-uc-system-proxy-shelf).

## Provenance
Akien dictation, 2026-04-19.

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/swarm_manager.py (new); lab/utility_closet/uc_registry.py (register); tests/test_swarm_manager.py; cross-cuts existing MachinesWatcher + machine_manager query call sites (migrate or wrap).

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (subsystem doc-block at top of swarm_manager.py); no-sqlite (state in Postgres or in-memory cache); test-plan-or-why-not.

**Scope boundary:** IN — fleet aggregation + add_box/bootstrap workflow + SystemProxy snapshot collection + best-routing-target API. Absorbs T-remote-igor-bootstrap. OUT — actual remote-execution layer (defer to per-OS install ticket); multi-datacenter federation.

**Test plan:** tests/test_swarm_manager.py — mocked SystemProxy snapshots from N boxes; assert aggregation, offline detection, best-routing-target selection. add_box bootstrap covered by remote-bootstrap subticket E2E.
